insights.title: Normalize stringified tool call arguments when resuming persisted conversations
insights.problem: Resumed/persisted assistant messages could contain tool call arguments stored as Unparsed strings; provider DTO builders expected JSON objects and would emit invalid requests.
insights.root_cause: No transformation was applied during context load to convert Unparsed string arguments to structured JSON for resumed sessions.
insights.solution: Introduce NormalizeToolCallArguments transformer that walks Context.messages and runs arguments.normalize() on each ToolCallFull to convert Unparsed -> Parsed (or fallback object). The transformer is inserted into orchestrator transformer pipeline.

insights.title: Apply content-keyed todo updates when summarizing tool calls
insights.problem: Summarizer previously matched todo updates by ID and replaced the whole list, which would break when the model sent content-keyed incremental updates.
insights.root_cause: Summary extraction used id-based maps (todo.id) and removed items by comparing after-ids, incompatible with content-keyed incoming items.
insights.solution: Updated summarizer to iterate input.todos items, treat Cancelled by content (retain removal), update existing items by content, or append new items with empty id (server-generated). Also updated extraction to compute TodoChange entries by matching content.
insights.lesson_learned: Summary codepaths must reflect the canonical tool contract; when the tool switches to content-keyed diffs, all places consuming tool input must be updated accordingly.

crates/forge_repo/src/provider/openai_re.short_description: Transformer adjusting Codex/Responses API requests to Codex backend quirks.

insights.title: Stop forcing low text verbosity for codex transformer
insights.problem: Transformer forcibly set response text verbosity to Low, overwriting user-specified verbosity and preventing richer outputs when needed.
insights.root_cause: Code always inserted/overrode a TextResponseParam.verbosity = Low regardless of existing request settings.
insights.solution: Removed code that forced text.verbosity = Some(Verbosity::Low); leave the request's text verbosity untouched while still configuring other fields (reasoning.effort = High). Adjusted/removed tests accordingly.
insights.lesson_learned: Don't hard-enforce provider-level parameters that override user/agent intent unless absolutely necessary; if you must, make it configurable and document the reason.

insights.title: Support Ping/Cost events with numeric-or-string cost fields
insights.problem: Some proxies send heartbeat 'ping' events carrying cost (sometimes as string). Previously these were unrecognized.
insights.root_cause: EventData enum didn't include a Ping variant with tolerant number/string cost.
insights.solution: Added StringOrF64 untagged enum and PingEvent struct; updated TryFrom to map Ping to ChatCompletionMessage with Usage containing cost. Added tests for numeric and string costs and unknown events.
insights.lesson_learned: Add tolerant parsing for proxy-specific heartbeat events to capture usage/cost information that may arrive outside normal message chunks.

crates/forge_domain/src/tools/call/args..short_description: Serialization helpers for tool call arguments

insights.title: Handle stringified/double-encoded tool call arguments on deserialize and normalize
insights.problem: Tool call arguments persisted by some providers (e.g., kimi-k2p5-turbo) arrive as a JSON string (double-encoded) or as malformed JSON strings. Previously these were stored as raw Unparsed strings and sent back to APIs as strings, causing 400 errors.
insights.root_cause: Deserialize implementation didn't attempt to repair or unwrap Value::String containing JSON; persisted conversations could therefore carry Unparsed string payloads.
insights.solution: Deserialize now checks for Value::String, attempts json_repair to parse/repair it and returns ToolCallArguments::Parsed when possible; added normalize() that promotes Unparsed -> Parsed or builds a fallback object {"_raw_content": "..."} so downstream code always receives structured JSON.

insights.title: Preserve raw content with fallback when repair fails
insights.problem: If repair of malformed JSON fails we previously risked losing information or returning a plain Unparsed string to parts of the pipeline expecting a JSON object.
insights.root_cause: Downstream request builders required structured JSON; an Unparsed string could still be serialized as a JSON string and break provider APIs.
insights.solution: On failed repair, normalize() creates a Parsed Value::Object with key _raw_content holding the original string so callers always get an object.

crates/forge_repo/src/provider/openai_re.short_description: OpenAI Responses repository for Codex/OpenAI providers

insights.title: Preserve full responses endpoint for 'compatible' providers and support conversation headers for Codex
insights.problem: Providers that configure a full Responses endpoint were being rewritten to base/v1/responses causing wrong endpoint. For Codex style providers conversation continuity headers were not included when a conversation id was available.
insights.root_cause: Special-case list of providers omitted OPENAI_RESPONSES_COMPATIBLE; get_headers lacked an API for conversation-scoped headers.
insights.solution: Treat OPENAI_RESPONSES_COMPATIBLE like CODEX/OPENCODE_ZEN and preserve configured path. Add get_headers_for_conversation(conversation_id: Option<&str>) that injects x-client-request-id and session_id for Codex when conversation id provided.
insights.lesson_learned: When supporting multiple similar provider types, centralize provider-family behavior checks; when adding context-specific headers, expose a variant of header builder that accepts the context (conversation id).

insights.title: Mark SSE transport parse errors as retryable for OpenAI Responses
insights.problem: SSE parse errors coming from transport layer should be retryable, while UTF8 parse errors are not.
insights.root_cause: eventsource_stream's EventStreamError variants mix transport-level errors and non-retryable parse errors. Previously all SSE parse errors were returned as non-retryable.
insights.solution: Wrapped SSE parse error into into_sse_parse_error which wraps transport errors into forge_domain::Error::Retryable and preserves other errors as non-retryable. Updated tests to assert behavior.
insights.lesson_learned: Differentiate retryability based on SSE error kind; treat transport-level failures as retryable.

insights.title: Support model listing for OpenAI Responses-compatible provider
insights.problem: Earlier code returned an empty model list for codex/OpenAI Responses provider.
insights.root_cause: OpenAI Responses repository lacked logic to fetch model lists configured via provider.models() URL or hardcoded list.
insights.solution: Implement models() to check provider.models(): if Hardcoded return the models, if Url then perform http_get, check status, deserialize to ListModelResponse, and convert to internal Model list. Added detailed error context and deserialization error messages.
insights.lesson_learned: Provider backends must respect provider configuration (models url or hardcoded). When performing HTTP fetches, include contextual error messages and validate HTTP status before parsing JSON.

crates/forge_domain/src/result_stream_ex.short_description: ResultStream extension trait for aggregating streaming results

insights.title: Apply merge semantics when assembling streaming messages
insights.problem: ResultStreamExt was accumulating partial usage events, which double-counted tokens for providers that reported cumulative values in deltas.
insights.root_cause: Code assumed partial usage values were incremental deltas and used accumulate instead of merge.
insights.solution: When processing streaming events, detect 'complete' usage (prompt+completion in a message) vs cost-only vs partial: use replacement for full usage, sum costs when only cost arrives, and use Usage::merge (max) for partial cumulative updates. Tests updated to reflect merged totals.
insights.lesson_learned: Streaming aggregator must be provider-aware: prefer idempotent merge rules (max) for cumulative counters to avoid double-counting.

insights.title: Handle mixed usage reporting and cost-only events robustly when combining message streams
insights.problem: Accumulating usage across streaming chunks could double-count in GLM-style streams (complete usage in each chunk) or lose cost when cost-only events arrived before complete usage.
insights.root_cause: Single accumulation logic replaced usage with latest regardless of semantics; cost-only events had zero tokens and needed special handling.
insights.solution: Detect complete usage (prompt_tokens > 0 && completion_tokens > 0) to replace existing usage, detect cost-only events (0 tokens + cost present) to set cost on usage without touching tokens, otherwise accumulate partial usage. Added tests: OpenAI-style final-chunk usage, GLM replacement, cost-only add, cost preserved when cost arrives before complete usage.
insights.lesson_learned: Streaming token/usage semantics vary: implement heuristics to decide when to accumulate vs replace, and ensure cost attribution is preserved across event ordering.

insights.title: Convert internal reasoning config to provider-specific reasoning_effort parameter
insights.problem: Some provider APIs (Requesty/OpenAI-compatible) expect reasoning_effort strings rather than a structured ReasoningConfig.
insights.root_cause: Mismatch between internal reasoning representation and external provider parameter shapes.
insights.solution: Added transformer SetReasoningEffort which maps ReasoningConfig to reasoning_effort string according to rules (enabled false -> none, effort precedence, budget->effort buckets, enabled true default medium) and removes original reasoning field.
insights.lesson_learned: When targeting multiple provider APIs, add an explicit transformation layer that maps internal strongly-typed config objects to provider-specific primitives. Include tests for mapping rules and edge cases.

insights.title: Sanitize tool/call IDs to conform to Anthropic/Vertex Anthropic requirements
insights.problem: Anthropic (Vertex Anthropic) requires tool call IDs to match ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$; IDs containing other characters caused 400 Bad Request errors.
insights.root_cause: Tool call IDs were generated or forwarded verbatim, possibly containing punctuation and other invalid characters.
insights.solution: Add SanitizeToolIds transformer which replaces invalid characters with underscores for both ToolUse.id and ToolResult.tool_use_id. Add tests covering various cases and integrate transformer into anthropic provider pipeline.

crates/forge_repo/src/provider/openai_re.short_description: OpenAI response parser converting SSE to domain types

insights.title: Preserve message phase and avoid duplicating reasoning text during stream-to-domain conversion
insights.problem: Phase labels from model responses and reasoning encrypted content were not preserved correctly in domain messages; streaming delta events caused duplication of reasoning text and final event needed to carry encrypted content.
insights.root_cause: Response conversion didn't map oai::MessagePhase into domain MessagePhase, and streaming conversion cleared reasoning_details entirely, losing encrypted content. Reasoning deltas already provided text/summary, so final event should preserve only encrypted content.
insights.solution: Implement IntoDomain for oai::MessagePhase -> MessagePhase mapping; preserve phase on Message when present. Implement retain_encrypted_reasoning_details(details) helper that filters reasoning details to keep only 'reasoning.encrypted' entries during streaming finalization. Ensure reasoning delta parts include item_id so encrypted parts can be correlated. Add tests validating phase preservation and reasoning filtering.
insights.lesson_learned: Streaming protocols often emit both delta content and final metadata; conversion must avoid duplicating emitted content while preserving metadata only available in final event (like encrypted blobs).

insights.title: Handle tool call arguments that arrive only in done event (Spark / GPT-5.3 Codex)
insights.problem: Some providers (Spark/gpt-5.3-codex-spark) send function arguments only in a single 'done' event without deltas. Previously code discarded done arguments if deltas had been seen or didn't emit done arguments when no deltas were present.
insights.root_cause: Logic assumed arguments would always stream via deltas and skipped done events unconditionally.
insights.solution: Track which output indices have received deltas (received_toolcall_deltas HashSet). When done arrives, emit arguments only if no deltas were seen for that output index. Introduced ToolCallIndex newtype to use as HashMap/HashSet key. Added tests to cover both scenarios and a Spark-style integration test.
insights.lesson_learned: Streaming protocols differ across providers; implement robust state machines that handle both incremental deltas and single-complete events. Record which inputs were partially/fully provided to avoid duplication.

insights.title: Simulate UI notifier acknowledgement in orchestrator spec runner
insights.problem: New notifier-based coordination required tests to simulate UI acknowledging ToolCallStart, otherwise orchestrator would deadlock waiting on notify.
insights.root_cause: Behavioral change in orchestrator required tests to mimic UI notification.
insights.solution: Updated test Runner loop to notify the notifier when it receives a ChatResponse::ToolCallStart, allowing orchestrator to proceed in tests.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding synchronization primitives, update test harness to emulate the other party's acknowledgement; otherwise tests will hang or fail.

insights.title: Switch test runner to expose ShellService mock outputs
insights.problem: Orchestrator tests needed to run shell commands but runner lacked ShellService mock plumbing.
insights.root_cause: Runner previously didn't implement ShellService for test harness.
insights.solution: Added ShellOutput queue to Runner, implemented ShellService for Runner returning queued outputs, and wired mock_shell_outputs into TestContext defaults.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding features that depend on external commands, augment test harness to supply deterministic mock outputs and include them in TestContext so tests remain hermetic.

exercised_by: crates/forge_app/src/orch_spec/orch_system_spec.rs (system prompt tests using mock shell output)

insights.title: Provider catalog updates (model ids and params)
insights.problem: Provider model IDs sometimes incorrect or missing; some provider URL param names needed rename/normalization.
insights.root_cause: Upstream provider naming/host param conventions changed; provider catalog needed updates to remain accurate.
insights.solution: Fix Claude Opus 4.6 model id, add GLM model entries, rename minimax host param to HOSTNAME and normalize url_param_vars layout.
insights.lesson_learned: Provider metadata must be kept in sync with external provider naming; design provider.json parsing to be tolerant to small renames to make migrations easier.

insights.title: Add new providers and update provider metadata (OpenCode Zen, Minimax, Kimi Coding, context lengths)
insights.problem: New backends and updated context lengths need to be reflected in provider metadata for selection and pipeline routing.
insights.root_cause: Provider ecosystem changes require central provider registration.
insights.solution: Added entries for opencode_zen, minimax, kimi_coding and adjusted context_length for some Claude variants and gpt-5.3-codex-spark. Also added custom_headers for providers where required.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep provider metadata authoritative and ensure new providers include required info (auth env var, url, models, custom headers). When adding providers, also update ProviderId enum, pipeline routing and any transformer logic that depends on provider IDs.

insights.title: Add openai_responses_compatible provider and update model catalog entries
insights.problem: Need to support a Responses-compatible provider and manage evolving model IDs/metadata.
insights.root_cause: New API variants and model consolidations require provider registry updates.
insights.solution: Added a provider entry openai_responses_compatible with response_type OpenAIResponses and models URL; also added and later consolidated/updated GPT-5.x model ids and context_length changes across commits (gpt-5.4 additions, then consolidation to gpt-5.4 and context_length updates to 272000).
insights.lesson_learned: Provider registry is a central authoritative source; changes to model IDs or provider response types must be coordinated with repo code that routes and lists models. Keep provider.json changes backward compatible where possible and include tests verifying the config.

insights.title: Add gpt-5.3-codex provider model to provider catalog
insights.problem: Catalog lacked new GPT-5.3 Codex entry resulting in unrecognized model IDs.
insights.root_cause: New model introduced upstream not added to embedded provider.json.
insights.solution: Inserted gpt-5.3-codex entry with metadata (context_length, tools_supported etc.).
insights.lesson_learned: Keep provider catalog up-to-date when adding new model families; provider.json is source-of-truth for supported model features.

insights.title: Add new provider model entry (gemini-3.1-pro-preview)
insights.problem: New provider model needed to be exposed to internal provider catalog.
insights.root_cause: Catalog lacked the new 'gemini-3.1-pro-preview' model metadata.
insights.solution: Added a new JSON entry describing the Gemini model with context_length and capability flags.

shell-plugin/doctor.zsh.description: A comprehensive zsh diagnostic script that checks zsh version, terminal, Forge installation, plugin loading, theme, dependencies (fzf, fd, bat), and keyboard/terminal settings across platforms. It prints pass/fail/warn messages with remediation instructions and is intended to help users debug common shell-plugin installation and configuration problems.

key_constructs.purpose: Prints formatted section headers

key_constructs.purpose: Compares semantic version strings

key_constructs.purpose: Environment variables and arrays inspected to determine plugin/theme state

reports missing dependencies and suggests installation steps
checks plugin loading order and theme integration
provides keyboard/terminal configuration guidance

insights.title: Adjust severity levels in doctor script
insights.problem: Some environment checks reported as failure when they should be warnings/informational (e.g., missing Oh My Zsh or fonts).
insights.root_cause: Doctor script used 'fail' for items that are non-fatal recommendations.
insights.solution: Downgraded several 'fail' results to 'warn' or 'instruction' messages and clarified prompts.
insights.lesson_learned: Health-check scripts should differentiate between fatal and advisory checks — avoid alarming users for non-fatal issues.

insights.title: Update installation hint in doctor script
insights.problem: Doctor script suggested npm i -g forgecode@latest which is outdated.
insights.root_cause: Installer moved to curl URL and docs/diagnostic script wasn't updated.
insights.solution: Changed displayed install instruction to use curl -fsSL https://forgecode.dev/cli | sh.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep diagnostics and displayed install instructions in sync with official install method.

shell-plugin/forge.plugin.zsh.short_description: Main zsh plugin loader that sources modular plugin components
shell-plugin/forge.plugin.zsh.description: Top-level zsh plugin entrypoint that sources the plugin's modular files (config, highlight, helpers, completions, action handlers, dispatcher and bindings) to assemble the Forge zsh integration at runtime. It centralizes plugin composition so other code can generate a single plugin blob for users to eval.

key_constructs.name: source "${0:A:h}/lib/config.zsh"
key_constructs.purpose: Loads configuration helpers

key_constructs.name: source "${0:A:h}/lib/dispatcher.zsh"
key_constructs.purpose: Loads main dispatcher and widget registration

key_constructs.name: source "${0:A:h}/lib/bindings.zsh"
key_constructs.purpose: Loads key bindings and widget registration

key_behaviors: initializes Forge zsh plugin by sourcing component scripts
registers completions, actions and key bindings for interactive shell

insights.title: Keep session-scoped active agent and conversation ID in shell plugin
insights.problem: Shell plugin relied on environment variables and global state (FORGE_CONVERSATION_ID, FORGE_ACTIVE_AGENT) exported to the environment; handling of agent/conversation was brittle and inconsistent across sessions.
insights.root_cause: Global exported env vars were modified by plugin in ways that clashed with session expectations and required the CLI to accept --aid/--cid flags.
insights.solution: Use internal plugin hashed variables (_FORGE_ACTIVE_AGENT and _FORGE_CONVERSATION_ID) to keep session-scoped state, default to 'forge', pass the agent to CLI via --agent flag in _forge_exec, and improve fzf selection outputs with delimiters and preview calls. Also updated behaviours for session info/show and conversation selection to set the plugin-local conversation id and print selection info. This avoids exporting env vars and makes session state local to the shell plugin.

shell-plugin/forge.setup.zsh.short_description: Managed .zshrc block inserted by `forge zsh setup`
shell-plugin/forge.setup.zsh.description: Auto-generated snippet that the setup command injects into a user's .zshrc; ensures required plugins are present and evaluates the Forge plugin and theme if not already loaded. This block is intended to be machine-managed and warns users not to edit it manually.
key_behaviors: ensures required zsh plugins are added to plugins array
loads Forge plugin and theme by evaluating generated outputs

shell-plugin/forge.theme.zsh.description: Defines a function that calls the Forge binary to retrieve a formatted right-prompt string (agent/model/token info) and appends it to RPROMPT when the theme is loaded. It exports flags/env to reflect per-session overrides so the prompt reflects the active session state.

key_constructs.name: _forge_prompt_info
key_constructs.purpose: Calls `forge zsh rprompt` with session env overrides to get RPROMPT content

key_behaviors: displays model/agent info and token counts in the zsh right prompt
invokes Forge CLI to render prompt content

insights.title: RPROMPT and PROMPT_SUBST handling across lazy-loaded contexts
insights.problem: When sourced from within a function (lazy-load), setopt/prompts may not apply globally, and RPROMPT set inside function can be local unless typeset -g is used.
insights.root_cause: Zsh scoping and lazy-loading interactions (plugin managers that eval within function scope).
insights.solution: Attempts to use emulate -R zsh -c 'setopt PROMPT_SUBST' and typeset -g for RPROMPT were added, but later reverted in some commits due to regressions; project added normalization and careful handling instead.
insights.lesson_learned: Small shell-scoped changes can have surprising side effects across plugin managers; test in multiple lazy-loaders and provide a conservative default.

shell-plugin/keyboard.zsh.description: Script that prints helpful keyboard shortcut documentation tailored to the user's platform (macOS/Linux/Windows) and current ZSH keymap (emacs or vi). It formats and aligns key descriptions for readability and offers guidance to run the doctor script for troubleshooting meta/Alt key behavior.

crates/forge_main/src/zsh/plugin.rs.description: Rust helpers that embed the shell-plugin files, generate a single consolidated zsh plugin (including Clap completions), produce the theme string, execute zsh helper scripts with streaming output, and manage insertion/update of a managed forge block inside a user's .zshrc with backup and marker validation. It abstracts cross-platform execution details (temp file on Windows) and ensures safe updates with backups and marker parsing.

key_constructs.name: ZSH_PLUGIN_LIB
key_constructs.purpose: Embedded directory of shell-plugin/lib files via include_dir

key_constructs.name: generate_zsh_plugin
key_constructs.purpose: Concatenates embedded zsh files, strips comments/empties, appends Clap completions, and returns plugin text

key_constructs.purpose: Injects or updates the managed forge block in ~/.zshrc, creating backups and validating markers

key_constructs.purpose: Represents parsed state of start/end markers in .zshrc (NotFound/Valid/Invalid)

key_constructs.name: ZshSetupResult
key_constructs.purpose: Result returned by setup_zsh_integration with message and optional backup path

key_behaviors: generates a consolidated zsh plugin including completions
generates theme script content for RPROMPT
executes embedded zsh helper scripts and streams output
safely inserts or updates a managed block in user's .zshrc with backups

insights.title: Zsh plugin script generation: typeset scoping changes and normalization
insights.problem: There were multiple attempts to change the `typeset` scope to global (-g) to support lazy-loading plugin managers. These changes were reverted in places because they caused regressions and exposed implementation tradeoffs.
insights.root_cause: Using typeset -g ensures variables are global even when eval'd inside functions (helps lazy-loading), but changes the visibility and can conflict with user expectations or shell plugin semantics; toggling between -g, -gh, and -h reflects attempts to balance persistence vs hiding variables.
insights.solution: Project experimented with typeset -g and -gh then reverted to safer non-global hidden declarations in later commits. The codebase also added normalization of embedded script content to remove CRLFs for Windows and created a path to write temp zsh scripts for Windows execution.
insights.lesson_learned: Shell plugin code needs cross-platform handling (CRLF vs LF), and changing global scoping for convenience (lazy-loading) can have regressions — prefer explicit handling (hidden, localized vars) and test in multiple shell manager scenarios.

insights.title: Normalize embedded shell scripts for Windows and use temp script for execution
insights.problem: include_str! embedded .zsh files on Windows may contain CRLF causing zsh parsing failures; passing long scripts via -c can mangle quotes on Windows/CreateProcess.
insights.root_cause: CRLF line endings and CreateProcess quoting rules on Windows.
insights.solution: Add normalize_script to strip CRLFs and create_temp_zsh_script to write and run a temporary file on Windows (use zsh -f <file>), keeping temp dir alive until child completes.

insights.title: Embed zsh plugin via include_dir and use forge_embed helper
insights.problem: rust-embed based iteration and API usage differed from include_dir; zsh generation needed to read embedded files and strip comments.
insights.root_cause: Transition from rust-embed to include_dir required changes to how files are enumerated and content is accessed.
insights.solution: Replaced RustEmbed-derived ZshPluginLib with static ZSH_PLUGIN_LIB include_dir Dir, used forge_embed::files iterator to traverse files, and updated test Mutex initialization to use LazyLock.
insights.lesson_learned: When replacing an embedding library adjust file iteration and access APIs; ensure tests using environment locking are updated to new LazyLock-based patterns.

crates/forge_repo/src/provider/chat.rs.short_description: Routes chat and model requests to provider-specific repositories and caches models
crates/forge_repo/src/provider/chat.rs.description: High-level chat repository that selects the correct provider backend (OpenAI, Codex/Responses, Anthropic, Bedrock, Google, OpenCode) based on provider configuration and response type. It also caches model lists on disk (with background refresh tasks) and aborts background refresh tasks on drop.

key_constructs.name: ForgeChatRepository
key_constructs.purpose: Top-level chat repository that holds router, model cache and background refresh guard

key_constructs.name: ProviderRouter
key_constructs.purpose: Contains concrete provider repositories and dispatches chat/models requests

key_constructs.name: ForgeChatRepository::models
key_constructs.purpose: Returns cached model list if available and triggers background refresh otherwise

key_constructs.name: ProviderRouter::chat
key_constructs.purpose: Dispatches chat requests to the correct provider implementation based on ProviderResponse

key_constructs.name: BgRefresh
key_constructs.purpose: Holds and aborts background refresh tasks (AbortHandle) when dropped

key_behaviors: routes chat requests to the appropriate provider implementation
fetches and caches provider model lists with background refresh
cancels background refreshes when repository is dropped

insights.title: Introduce ProviderRouter and model caching background refresh
insights.problem: Chat repository mixed routing logic, model caching, and background refresh duties in a brittle way.
insights.root_cause: Originally multiple repos were fields on the top-level repository with ad-hoc routing; model list caching was simplistic or missing.
insights.solution: Introduce ProviderRouter to centralize routing (chat/models). Add a model_cache (CacacheStorage) used by models() to return cached values fast and trigger a background refresh task. Implement BgRefresh guard to cancel background tasks on drop. models() now returns cached list when available and refreshes in background; otherwise fetches and caches synchronously.
insights.lesson_learned: Separate routing concerns into a small router struct and keep caching isolated. When doing background refreshes, track abort handles and ensure they are cancelled on drop to avoid runaway tasks. Be careful to avoid silent failures: log background refresh errors with context.

insights.title: Route OpenAIResponses providers to codex_repo for chat and models
insights.problem: OpenAI Responses-compatible providers need to use the codex repo for chat/models; earlier routing did not include the responses variant.
insights.root_cause: ProviderResponse variants added required routing to map OpenAIResponses to existing codex repo implementation.
insights.solution: Add match arms mapping ProviderResponse::OpenAIResponses to codex_repo for chat() and models().
insights.lesson_learned: When adding provider response variants, ensure the central router maps them to the appropriate implementation.

crates/forge_app/src/agent_executor.rs.short_description: Executes agents as tools and provides agent tool definitions
crates/forge_app/src/agent_executor.rs.description: AgentExecutor provides functions to list agent tool definitions and to execute an agent as a tool call by creating (or reusing) a conversation, invoking the Forge application chat workflow, streaming responses, and converting the final output into a ToolOutput element. It also caches agent definitions and exposes a contains_tool helper.

key_constructs.name: AgentExecutor
key_constructs.purpose: Encapsulates services and cached tool agent definitions

key_constructs.name: AgentExecutor::agent_definitions
key_constructs.purpose: Returns cached or freshly loaded ToolDefinition list for available agents

key_constructs.name: AgentExecutor::execute
key_constructs.purpose: Runs an agent as a tool call, streams chat responses, and returns ToolOutput

key_constructs.name: AgentExecutor::contains_tool
key_constructs.purpose: Checks whether a given tool name is provided by any agent

key_behaviors: exposes agent tool definitions for tool registry
executes an agent as an LLM-powered tool and collects output
reuses or creates conversations for agent-invoked tasks

insights.title: AgentExecutor supports conversation reuse (session_id) for subagents
insights.problem: Subagents needed to either resume an existing agent conversation or start a fresh one; previously agent execution always created a new conversation.
insights.root_cause: AgentExecutor.execute created a new conversation unconditionally.
insights.solution: Add optional ConversationId parameter to execute. If provided, find_conversation and reuse it (error if missing); otherwise create a conversation with context.initiator("agent") and upsert it. This enables resumption of prior agent sessions and proper billing/metadata.
insights.lesson_learned: Allow agent tool calls to optionally reuse conversation context to preserve continuity; always mark agent-initiated conversations appropriately.

insights.title: Pattern-match ChatResponse::ToolCallStart variant fields correctly
insights.problem: Code matched deprecated tuple-style enum variant patterns leading to compile-time mismatches after ChatResponse variant changed to struct-like.
insights.root_cause: ChatResponse ToolCallStart changed shape earlier; agent_executor wasn't updated to struct-style match patterns.
insights.solution: Updated matches to use ChatResponse::ToolCallStart { .. } pattern and similar for send/clear behavior.
insights.lesson_learned: When changing enum variant representation (tuple -> struct-like), update all pattern matches. Prefer using { .. } when only presence matters.

insights.title: AgentExecutor.execute now takes AgentId instead of String
insights.problem: Multiple callsites expected the old signature; sub-agent calls required strong AgentId typing.
insights.root_cause: Refactor to strengthen types and avoid passing raw strings as agent identifiers.
insights.solution: Change execute signature to accept AgentId; update internal chat call to pass agent id in ChatRequest and ForgeApp.chat.

crates/forge_app/src/user_prompt.rs.short_description: Generates and injects user prompts and attachments into conversations
crates/forge_app/src/user_prompt.rs.description: UserPromptGenerator renders the user's prompt (using templates and event context), injects it into the conversation context, optionally adds droppable 'piped' context and todos when resuming, parses attachments found in the prompt and records file-read metrics. It's responsible for preparing the conversation state that will be sent to the chat model.

key_constructs.name: UserPromptGenerator::add_attachments
key_constructs.purpose: Parses attachments from rendered content, adds them to context, and records file read metrics

key_constructs.name: UserPromptGenerator::add_todos_on_resume
key_constructs.purpose: Injects existing todos as a droppable user message when resuming a conversation

key_behaviors: renders and injects user prompts into conversation context
adds piped input and todos as droppable messages when appropriate
parses attachments and records file-read operations in metrics

insights.title: Use FileInfo for attachments and account for cancelled todos
insights.problem: Attachment handling and metrics used per-range fields and content_hash; with new FileInfo shape and content-keyed todos the code needed to adapt.
insights.root_cause: File read output shape changed (now ReadOutput.info: FileInfo). Also todo updates are content-keyed so metrics updates need to refer to FileInfo.content_hash.
insights.solution: Updated code that constructs metrics to read FileInfo from AttachmentContent::FileContent and store FileOperation.content_hash from info.content_hash. Also added display string for TodoStatus::Cancelled in prompt generator.
insights.lesson_learned: When internal representations change (consolidated metadata structs), update all callers. Keep prompt generation consistent with domain enum additions.

insights.title: Use raw content_hash from attachment rather than recomputing from displayed content
insights.problem: UserPromptGenerator previously recomputed content-based metric hashes from displayed (line-numbered) content, causing mismatch with raw-file hash used by external-change detector.
insights.root_cause: The metrics tracked file content hash using formatted/display content instead of a raw content hash present in attachment metadata.
insights.solution: Use AttachmentContent::FileContent.content_hash (raw hash) when populating conversation metrics; updated tests to include content_hash fields.

insights.title: Inject existing todos into resumed conversations
insights.problem: When resuming a conversation, the assistant lacked immediate visibility of existing todos in the prompt which could cause context loss.
insights.root_cause: Resumption flow added messages but didn't re-inject todos as droppable user messages.
insights.solution: On resume, detect prior user messages and inject a droppable user message containing the current todo list formatted as markdown; added add_todos_on_resume and format_todos_as_markdown helper functions. Added tests verifying injection on resume and no injection on new conversations.
insights.lesson_learned: Long-lived session state (todos) should be surfaced into resumed contexts in a droppable/non-authoritative way to preserve context without polluting canonical history. Tests should cover both resume and new-conversation flows.

crates/forge_main/src/lib.rs.short_description: Top-level forge_main crate module and public exports
crates/forge_main/src/lib.rs.description: Declares and imports the submodules used by the forge CLI/TUI crate, re-exports key types (Cli, Sandbox, UI, title display utilities) and initializes a global tracker LazyLock. Acts as the central module aggregator for the forge_main crate.

insights.title: ConfigManager export removed; config module deleted
insights.problem: Previously public ConfigManager was exported from lib.rs and consumed elsewhere
insights.root_cause: Config logic was inlined into UI and config module files were deleted
insights.solution: Remove pub use config::ConfigManager; update callers to use UI::handle_config_command
insights.lesson_learned: Deleting a module and its public types is a breaking change. Ensure dependent crates or integration points are updated simultaneously; consider a deprecation bridge where needed.

It wraps provider responses with retry/enhanced error context and implements the ChatRepository interface.

insights.title: Support provider-level custom_headers and include them in requests
insights.problem: Some providers require additional headers (e.g., custom User-Agent) at provider level; current provider template didn't carry custom headers.
insights.root_cause: provider.json and Provider template lacked a custom_headers field.
insights.solution: Added custom_headers Option<HashMap<String,String>> to Provider template, propagated through provider repo and services. OpenAI provider get_headers appends provider.custom_headers entries to request headers. Tests added to verify header injection.
insights.lesson_learned: Allow per-provider custom headers to handle proxies or vendor-specific requirements. Ensure default header insertion logic respects provider-provided values (e.g., don't override a provider User-Agent).

insights.title: Enhance GitHub Copilot model-not-supported error message
insights.problem: Errors from GitHub Copilot about disabled models returned cryptic provider errors; users couldn't easily find remediation steps.
insights.root_cause: Generic error messages were propagated without provider-specific context.
insights.solution: Added enhance_error() which inspects error messages for model_not_supported patterns for ProviderId::GITHUB_COPILOT and appends helpful context including a link. Wrapped http_eventsource error mapping with map_err to call enhance_error. Also applied enhance_error when mapping stream items with into_retry.
insights.lesson_learned: Provider-specific errors should be enriched with actionable guidance; wrap map_err at call sites and include provider id context. Add snapshot tests to ensure message formatting.

It also provides conversion to ToolDefinition and helper methods for compaction and tool ordering.

It standardizes GET/POST/DELETE/eventsource flows and writes request payloads to disk when debug_paths are configured.

configures TLS and root certificates from config
optionally writes request payloads to a debug file

insights.title: Consolidate header sanitization and expand sensitive list
insights.problem: Multiple providers had ad-hoc sanitize_headers implementations and the set of sensitive headers was incomplete.
insights.root_cause: Utility duplication across crates and incomplete list (only Authorization previously).
insights.solution: Moved sanitize_headers into forge_infra::http module (public re-export) and expanded sensitive headers to include x-api-key, x-goog-api-key, api-key, etc. Added unit test test_sanitize_headers_redacts_sensitive_values.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize logging/PII redaction utilities in infra layer, keep the sensitive header list broad (authorization, API keys), and add tests to prevent regressions.

insights.title: Append debug request body chunks instead of overwriting
insights.problem: When streaming request bodies were logged to debug files, later chunks overwrote earlier ones causing truncated logs.
insights.root_cause: The debug path writer used write (overwrite) instead of append.
insights.solution: Switch to file_writer.append(...) when writing debug request body chunks; implement FileWriterInfra::append and underlying impls in ForgeFileWriteService and ForgeFS.
insights.lesson_learned: For streaming debug captures, append semantics are required; ensure underlying FS service exposes append and tests simulate append behavior.

insights.title: Write debug POST/requests bodies to file when debug_requests configured
insights.problem: Debugging remote provider interactions required capturing raw request bodies. Existing code duplicated logic for POST/eventsource handling.
insights.root_cause: No single helper to conditionally write debug request bodies and repeated code paths.
insights.solution: Introduced write_debug_request helper and invoked it in post() and eventsource() paths. Spawn write task to file writer. Added tests to assert file writes occur when debug_requests path present.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize debug/log writing logic to avoid duplication and ensure uniform behavior across HTTP modes.

crates/forge_select/src/select.rs.short_description: Interactive fuzzy select builder using fzf
crates/forge_select/src/select.rs.description: Implements a SelectBuilder that wraps fzf to present interactive fuzzy-search selection and confirm prompts on the terminal. It formats items with an index prefix to avoid ambiguities, strips ANSI sequences for matching, and supports confirm prompts mapped to generic types.

key_constructs.name: SelectBuilder

key_constructs.name: build_fzf
key_constructs.purpose: Configures an Fzf instance with flags and optional header/query/cursor

key_constructs.name: SelectBuilder::prompt
key_constructs.purpose: Runs the fzf prompt and returns the selected option or None if cancelled

key_behaviors: presents fuzzy-search selection lists inline using fzf
provides yes/no confirmation prompts via fzf
maps fzf output back to original option indices

insights.title: Replace unsafe transmute_copy with safe Any downcast for confirm prompt
insights.problem: Code used unsafe std::mem::transmute_copy to convert a bool result to generic type T, which is undefined behavior / memory-unsafe.
insights.root_cause: Unsafe mem transmute used to coerce types instead of safe runtime type check and downcasting.
insights.solution: Introduce prompt_confirm returning Option<bool> and prompt_confirm_as wrapper which verifies TypeId and safely downcasts via Box<dyn Any>. Eliminates transmute_copy usage.

insights.title: Replace dialoguer-based selection UI with fzf-wrapped integration
insights.problem: dialoguer's fuzzy select had limitations/maintenance issues; desire to match shell plugin/fzf behavior and support header-lines/starting cursor.
insights.root_cause: UI consistency across CLI and shell plugin required fzf-like behavior (header rows, starting cursor, column display).
insights.solution: Introduce fzf-wrapped selection builder API (via fzf-wrapped crate), remove terminal.rs and crossterm dependencies, adapt select internals to support starting_cursor, header_lines and more porcelain-friendly display. Many callers updated to provide header and row semantics.

crates/forge_services/src/lib.rs.description: Top-level module for forge_services that declares submodules and re-exports common service types. It also defines generic traits IntoDomain and FromDomain used across services to convert between external representations and domain models.

crates/forge_app/src/dto/openai/request..description: Defines the request-side DTOs (Message, Request, ContentPart, Tool, etc.) used to communicate with OpenAI/compatible providers and implements conversions from domain types (Context, ContextMessage, ToolCallFull). It also handles caching flags, response format mapping, and safe serialization/normalization of tool arguments.

key_behaviors: converts domain chat contexts into provider-compatible request payloads
normalizes and serializes tool call arguments to JSON schema
supports message caching annotations and streaming options

insights.title: Handle double-encoded tool call arguments when serializing OpenAI-style requests
insights.problem: Tool call arguments could be double-encoded (string containing JSON) or not strictly conforming to a tool's input schema; naive serde_json::to_string(&value.arguments) would re-serialize the original string producing a JSON string payload for the API.
insights.root_cause: Serialization didn't attempt to parse/normalize argument strings or coerce them to the tool's declared schema.
insights.solution: Add serialize_tool_call_arguments(tool_call) which parses the arguments, attempts to coerce to the tool's input_schema via forge_json_repair::coerce_to_schema (ToolCatalog lookup), and serializes the normalized object. Fall back to original serialization when parsing/coercion fails.

insights.title: Add reasoning_content flat alias for Kimi K2 compatibility
insights.problem: Some backends like kimi_k2 expect a flat reasoning_content field rather than reasoning_details arrays.
insights.root_cause: Different model providers have distinct schema expectations.
insights.solution: Added reasoning_content Option<String> alias to Message struct and ensured transformers clear/populate it as needed.
insights.lesson_learned: When supporting multiple provider backends, make DTOs flexible and add explicit fields or aliases used by transformers for interop.

crates/forge_repo/src/conversation/conve.description: Defines storage-focused record types for conversation persistence and conversions to/from forge_domain types, including legacy compatibility migrations for older record variants. This keeps the repository layer decoupled from domain changes while providing TryFrom/From implementations for safe serialization/deserialization.

key_behaviors: serializes conversation components for storage
deserializes stored records into domain types with legacy migrations
encapsulates storage schema independent of domain model

insights.title: Add legacy ToolValueRecord variants and migration paths
insights.problem: Old persisted conversations used variants (Markdown, FileDiff, Pair) removed from current domain model; deserializing these caused failures.
insights.root_cause: Domain model evolved but stored data still contains older variants; no migration code existed in repository layer.
insights.solution: Added legacy variants Markdown, FileDiff, Pair and FileDiffRecord struct, and TryFrom impls to convert legacy variants into current forge_domain::ToolValue equivalents (e.g., Markdown -> Text, FileDiff -> Text summary, Pair -> use first element).
insights.lesson_learned: When evolving serialized domain enums keep repository-level compatibility code that recognizes legacy variants and converts them to current domain types; add unit tests exercising legacy JSON blobs.

crates/forge_app/src/changed_files.rs.short_description: Detects externally modified files and injects notifications
crates/forge_app/src/changed_files.rs.description: Service that detects file changes external to the agent using a FileChangeDetector, updates conversation metrics to avoid duplicate notices, and inserts a droppable user message summarizing changed files. It formats display paths relative to cwd and updates file hashes in the conversation metrics.

key_constructs.name: ChangedFiles
key_constructs.purpose: Service struct holding infra and agent info used to detect and notify about changed files

key_constructs.name: ChangedFiles::update_file_stats
key_constructs.purpose: Detects changed files, updates metrics hashes, and appends a droppable notification message to the conversation

key_constructs.name: FileChangeDetector
key_constructs.purpose: Used to detect file modifications by comparing recorded hashes to current file states (used via crate::file_tracking)

key_behaviors: detects externally modified files against tracked hashes
updates conversation metrics with new file hashes
inserts a user-facing, droppable notification listing modified files

insights.title: Use dynamic config for parallel file reads
insights.problem: The number of parallel file reads was previously passed in from callers or read from a cached config; when config changed the detectors might still use the old value.
insights.root_cause: Parallel read config was threaded instead of being fetched where needed.
insights.solution: Changed update_file_stats to call services.get_config().map(|c| c.max_parallel_file_reads).unwrap_or(4) and FileChangeDetector.detect now accepts parallel_file_reads parameter. Tests updated to call new signature.
insights.lesson_learned: Small concurrency/throughput knobs should be fetched dynamically; update tests when signatures change.

insights.title: Use FileInfo for ReadOutput in changed files tests
insights.problem: Tests constructing ReadOutput used separate fields; code now expects info: FileInfo.
insights.root_cause: Migration to FileInfo required test updates.
insights.solution: Updated tests to populate ReadOutput.info with FileInfo::new(..., content_hash).
insights.lesson_learned: When changing public DTO shapes, update unit tests to validate the new shape; snapshots must be consistent with new fields.

insights.title: Pass parallel_file_reads configuration into FileChangeDetector
insights.problem: ChangedFiles triggered file detection without controlling concurrency.
insights.root_cause: FileChangeDetector gained a new constructor but callers were not updated to pass concurrency.
insights.solution: Changed update_file_stats to pass self.services.get_environment().parallel_file_reads into FileChangeDetector::new.
insights.lesson_learned: When introducing a concurrency tuning parameter, update all construction sites and surface it through environment/service layers.

exercised_by: crates/forge_app/src/changed_files.rs (mod tests adjusted to new signature)

crates/forge_app/src/system_prompt.rs.description: Constructs the system-level prompt content for an Agent by rendering templates with environment, tool and file context, and optional custom rules/skills. It can fetch git-based extension statistics, prepare tool information dependent on model capabilities, and append system messages to conversations.

insights.title: Add file extension statistics to system prompt using git ls-files
insights.problem: System prompt lacked quick summary of repository file-type composition which is useful for system-level context.
insights.root_cause: SystemContext didn't include extension statistics and there was no service hook to run git to gather stats.
insights.solution: Added ShellService dependency to SystemPrompt, implemented fetch_extensions that runs git ls-files via ShellService, added parse_extensions to summarize extension counts/percentages, extended SystemContext to include Extension data, updated templates to render workspace_extensions partial, and added tests plus fixtures to validate behavior and truncation to top-N extensions.
insights.lesson_learned: Adding environment context via shell commands is useful but must handle failures (non-git repos) gracefully; keep summary sizes bounded (max_extensions) and ensure templates and tests reflect the new context.

It centralizes startup validation (config and services URL) and sandbox/worktree selection before handing control to the interactive or one-shot UI.

insights.title: Enable VT processing on STD_OUTPUT_HANDLE on Windows for ANSI rendering
insights.problem: ANSI escape sequences were shown as raw bytes on Windows in some console configurations because VT processing was not enabled on the STD_OUTPUT_HANDLE; the enable_ansi_support crate sets CONOUT$ but flags are per-handle and may not propagate.
insights.root_cause: Console mode flags are per-handle on Windows; enabling VT on the CONOUT$ screen buffer isn't always sufficient for the standard stdout handle.
insights.solution: Add enable_stdout_vt_processing() that calls GetStdHandle(STD_OUTPUT_HANDLE), GetConsoleMode, and SetConsoleMode to OR in ENABLE_VIRTUAL_TERMINAL_PROCESSING (only on Windows). Keep stderr unchanged to avoid breaking console crate spinner behavior.

insights.title: Thread CLI model/provider flags into ForgeAPI init
insights.problem: New CLI flags needed to be passed into API construction.
insights.root_cause: ForgeAPI::init signature didn't accept runtime overrides, so flags couldn't be used at session start.
insights.solution: Modified ForgeAPI::init to accept override_model and override_provider and capture cli.model/cli.provider into the closure passed to UI::init.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding global CLI-level overrides, ensure initialization closures capture and forward values to downstream dependency constructors.

crates/forge_repo/src/skill.rs.short_description: Skill repository loading built-in, global and project skills
crates/forge_repo/src/skill.rs.description: Implements ForgeSkillRepository which loads builtin embedded skills, then global and project-local skills from disk, resolves conflicts by precedence (CWD > Global > Built-in), and renders skill commands with environment paths. It reads SKILL.md front matter (YAML) to extract metadata and gathers resource files in each skill directory.

key_constructs.name: ForgeSkillRepository
key_constructs.purpose: Repository implementation that loads skills from builtin, global and local directories via infra

key_constructs.name: ForgeSkillRepository::load_builtin_skills
key_constructs.purpose: Returns a list of embedded built-in skill definitions

key_constructs.name: ForgeSkillRepository::load_skills_from_dir
key_constructs.purpose: Asynchronously enumerates subdirectories, reads SKILL.md and collects resources for each skill

key_constructs.purpose: Parses YAML front matter from SKILL.md to extract skill name and description

key_constructs.name: resolve_skill_conflicts
key_constructs.purpose: Resolves duplicate skill names keeping the last occurrence (preference ordering)

key_behaviors: loads skills from built-in, global and project-local locations
parses SKILL.md front matter to extract metadata
resolves duplicate skills giving CWD precedence
renders skill commands with environment paths

insights.title: Avoid treating skill content as full template
insights.problem: Skill command content was being rendered through a full template engine causing unintended template evaluation and errors when the skill text contained template-like sequences.
insights.root_cause: TemplateEngine::render_template was used on arbitrary skill.command content, but skills are expected to support only a couple of simple substitutions.
insights.solution: Replace template engine rendering with simple .replace of known placeholders ({{global_skills_path}} and {{local_skills_path}}) preserving literal skill content elsewhere.
insights.lesson_learned: Avoid over-eager templating on free-form user content — prefer explicit, minimal substitutions to avoid accidental interpretation of template syntax.

insights.title: Rename skill path and name from pr-description to github-pr-description
insights.problem: Skill naming not explicit about GitHub context.
insights.root_cause: Skill name 'pr-description' ambiguous; renaming to github-pr-description clarifies provider target.
insights.solution: Rename skill file path and name constant; update references and tests.
insights.lesson_learned: Rename resources to be explicit about ecosystem (GitHub) to reduce ambiguity. Update fixtures and tests referencing the path.

insights.title: Embed new built-in PR description skill
insights.problem: Built-in skills list didn't include the new pr-description skill and tests expected the list size to increase.
insights.root_cause: New skill files were added/renamed (.forge -> commands and skills embedded in repo) but the skill registry wasn't updated.
insights.solution: Added an entry for forge://skills/pr-description/SKILL.md into the embedded skills array and extended tests to assert presence.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding/renaming embedded skill files update the skill registry and corresponding tests; keep skill path constants consistent (forge:// prefix).

exercised_by: crates/forge_repo/src/skill.rs (inline tests updated in same file)

insights.title: Treat Google ADC marker across Vertex-like providers
insights.problem: Code only treated VERTEX_AI provider specially for Google ADC marker; other Vertex variants (e.g., VERTEX_AI_ANTHROPIC) were not recognized and would attempt incorrect auth flows.
insights.root_cause: Conditional check compared provider_id == VERTEX_AI only; a second provider identifier representing Vertex Anthropic existed but wasn't included.
insights.solution: Define is_vertex_provider boolean checking both VERTEX_AI and VERTEX_AI_ANTHROPIC then use it in the condition to detect the 'google_adc_marker'. Expand match arms to include AuthMethod::GoogleAdc in places that previously only recognized CodexDevice etc.
insights.lesson_learned: When multiple provider identifiers represent similar auth flows, centralize detection logic (is_vertex_provider) to avoid missing variants. Also include new AuthMethod variants into match arms consistently.

It groups reasoning details for replay, normalizes schemars JSON schemas to OpenAI-compatible tool parameters, and ensures streaming/encrypted reasoning content is requested when necessary.

includes reasoning encrypted content when reasoning is enabled
converts tool schemas into strict JSON Schema accepted by the Responses API

insights.title: Preserve message phase and request encrypted reasoning content when needed
insights.problem: Phase labels from messages (commentary vs final_answer) were not preserved when building requests; reasoning encrypted content was not requested leading to missing data for stateless reasoning replay.
insights.root_cause: Mapping to OpenAI request types omitted message.phase and didn't include ReasoningEncryptedContent include when reasoning was configured.
insights.solution: Add mapping from domain MessagePhase to oai::MessagePhase; set phase on assistant EasyMessage items when present; when response.reasoning.is_some() ensure include contains ReasoningEncryptedContent to receive encrypted reasoning data for replay.
insights.lesson_learned: When supporting advanced response metadata (phases, encrypted blobs) ensure both outgoing requests ask for the content and incoming response conversion preserves the metadata.

insights.title: ToolCallStart now carries notifier; enum style normalized
insights.problem: ToolCallStart previously carried ToolCallFull directly; needed to carry notifier to coordinate UI/tool execution.
insights.root_cause: UI/tool stdout interleaving fix needed a way to notify orchestration when UI header is rendered.
insights.solution: Replaced ChatResponse::ToolCallStart(ToolCallFull) with ChatResponse::ToolCallStart { tool_call: ToolCallFull, notifier: Arc<Notify> }. Also normalized enum variant formatting and imports.
insights.lesson_learned: When changing message types used across async tasks, prefer explicit structured variants so additional coordination fields can be added without awkward API ripples.

insights.title: Allow agent to define provider (per-agent provider)
insights.problem: Agents could not specify their own provider; the system assumed a single default provider.
insights.root_cause: Agent struct didn't have an optional provider field to override the default.
insights.solution: Add provider: Option<ProviderId> to Agent struct and merge/serde support. AgentService chat methods now accept provider_id Option and resolve to per-agent provider or default provider internally. This enables per-agent provider resolution throughout ForgeApp.

crates/forge_app/src/file_tracking.rs.short_description: Detects external file changes by comparing stored and current hashes
crates/forge_app/src/file_tracking.rs.description: Provides a FileChangeDetector that reads current file hashes using an FsReadService and compares them with tracked hashes in Metrics to detect modifications, deletions, or unreadable files. The detector parallelizes reads, returns sorted change lists, and includes comprehensive tests with a mock FS service to avoid false positives from truncated display content.

key_constructs.name: FileChange
key_constructs.purpose: Represents a detected change with path and optional new content_hash

key_constructs.name: FileChangeDetector
key_constructs.purpose: Detects file changes by reading current content hashes and comparing with tracked Metrics

key_constructs.name: FileChangeDetector::detect
key_constructs.purpose: Asynchronously check files in Metrics, parallelized, and return sorted changes

key_constructs.name: MockFsReadService
key_constructs.purpose: Test double that simulates reading files and producing raw/display content/hash

key_behaviors: detect external modifications to files
avoid false positives when displayed content is truncated
return deterministic sorted change lists

insights.title: FileChangeDetector parallelism parameter made explicit
insights.problem: FileChangeDetector stored a parallelism field and was constructed with that value; after moving config resolution into services the signature needed to accept the concurrency value at detect time.
insights.root_cause: Configuration movement/refactor required API signature changes to avoid holding stale config in the detector.
insights.solution: Removed parallel_file_reads from struct, changed new() and detect() to accept parallel_file_reads on detect, and tests updated accordingly.
insights.lesson_learned: When a service depends on dynamic config, avoid embedding config into long-lived structs unless it's immutable; prefer passing runtime parameters at call time.

insights.title: Compare stored file hashes to FileInfo.content_hash from range_read
insights.problem: External change detector read and compared content hashes that didn't match stored hashes because stored hash might be from different content computation.
insights.root_cause: ReadOutput.content_hash field was replaced by ReadOutput.info.content_hash; callers still expected previous field.
insights.solution: Updated FileChangeDetector to use output.info.content_hash. Tests updated to assert FileInfo in ReadOutput.
insights.lesson_learned: When consolidating data into a nested struct, update all callsites and tests; this avoids silent mismatches.

insights.title: Cap parallel file reads to avoid EMFILE (too many open files)
insights.problem: Unbounded parallel reads could exhaust file descriptors (EMFILE) when hashing many files concurrently.
insights.root_cause: detect() created an unbounded set of futures and awaited join_all, causing many simultaneous file opens.
insights.solution: Switch to streaming over entries and use buffer_unordered(n) with a configured parallel_file_reads cap; collect with filter_map and preserve deterministic sorting. Accept parallel_file_reads in FileChangeDetector::new and propagate via environment.
insights.lesson_learned: I/O-heavy parallel operations must be bounded; expose a configurable concurrency cap via environment and use StreamExt::buffer_unordered to limit concurrency and avoid system resource exhaustion.

exercised_by: crates/forge_app/src/file_tracking.rs (unit tests updated to pass parallel_file_reads)

crates/forge_config/src/reader.rs.description: Builds ForgeConfig by layering embedded defaults, global user files, legacy JSON, provided TOML strings, and FORGE_ environment variables, while loading .env files from the working directory chain once per process. It centralizes configuration resolution and exposes helper methods for config path discovery.

crates/forge_domain/src/file.rs.short_description: Domain models for file metadata, hashes and sync statuses
crates/forge_domain/src/file.rs.description: Defines domain structs representing files and file read metadata (File, FileInfo), server file hashes (FileHash), and synchronization enums/structs (SyncStatus, FileStatus). These models are used to coordinate workspace syncing, change detection and reporting across services.

key_constructs.name: File
key_constructs.purpose: Represents a path and whether it's a directory

key_constructs.name: FileInfo
key_constructs.purpose: Describes a read operation, lines range, total lines, and full content hash

key_constructs.name: FileHash
key_constructs.purpose: Represents server file path and SHA-256 hash

key_constructs.name: SyncStatus
key_constructs.purpose: Possible sync states: InSync, Modified, New, Deleted, Failed

key_constructs.name: FileStatus
key_constructs.purpose: A path with its computed SyncStatus

key_behaviors: model file metadata and sync states
store full-file content hash for reliable change detection

insights.title: FileInfo extended with content_hash (full-file SHA-256)
insights.problem: External-change detector hashes full file content; range readers used partial/truncated content hashes which could cause false positives.
insights.root_cause: Inconsistent hashing: callers sometimes hashed the returned range content leading to mismatches with external detectors that hash the full file
insights.solution: Added content_hash: String to FileInfo and updated constructor to accept it. FileInfo is now Serialize/Deserialize/Eq to support storage and comparisons.
insights.lesson_learned: When external systems perform equality checks, ensure your metadata represents the same canonical substrate (full-file hash) and make that explicit in types.

crates/forge_domain/src/session_metrics..short_description: Session metrics storage for file operations, todos and timing
crates/forge_domain/src/session_metrics..description: Holds metrics collected during a session including last file operations, accessed files, todos and start time. Provides helper methods to record file operations (tracking reads separately), compute session duration, and apply incoming todo changes while validating constraints.

key_constructs.name: Metrics
key_constructs.purpose: Primary session metrics container for file_operations, files_accessed, todos and start time

key_constructs.name: Metrics::insert
key_constructs.purpose: Record a file operation and add reads to files_accessed set

key_constructs.name: Metrics::apply_todo_changes
key_constructs.purpose: Merge incoming TodoItem changes into stored todos with validation and history rules

key_constructs.name: Metrics::get_active_todos
key_constructs.purpose: Return todos with Pending or InProgress status

key_behaviors: record file operations and mark files accessed
manage todo lifecycle and validate incoming changes
compute session duration

insights.title: Apply content-keyed todo changes and introduce TodoItem type
insights.problem: Old update_todos relied on IDs being provided by the client and validated uniqueness; this conflicted with the new model where server manages IDs and updates are content-keyed.
insights.root_cause: Protocol changed: model now sends only changed items keyed by 'content' and can send 'cancelled' status to indicate removal. Server-side API still expected client-provided IDs.
insights.solution: Replaced update_todos(Vec<Todo>) with apply_todo_changes(Vec<TodoItem>) that validates content, treats 'cancelled' as removal by content, updates existing items by matching content, and generates server UUIDs for new items. Ensured completed items not mentioned remain in history and only active items are returned.
insights.lesson_learned: When the wire format changes (content-keyed diffs), adapt server-side data structures and validation accordingly; avoid requiring client-managed IDs for server-managed entities. Provide clear validation errors for empty/oversized content.

insights.title: Preserve historical completed todos when updating session todos
insights.problem: When update_todos replaced the session todos vector, completed todos that had been removed from the active list were lost from metrics (no historical trace). Also callers expected update_todos to return active todos.
insights.root_cause: update_todos blindly replaced the internal vector; there was no distinction between active todos and historical completed todos.
insights.solution: Introduce merging logic: keep incoming active todos and re-append any previously-known completed todos that are not present in the new active list; sort by id for deterministic ordering. Add get_active_todos() to return only pending/in-progress items while get_todos() returns all known todos.
insights.lesson_learned: Metrics that are append-only or partially historical must be explicit about what 'replace' semantics mean. Provide separate accessors for active vs historical items and ensure mutating methods preserve history where required.

insights.title: Add unit test verifying removed completed todos are preserved
insights.problem: Lack of tests to capture history-preservation expectations caused earlier regressions.
insights.root_cause: No test coverage for update_todos history behaviour.
insights.solution: Added test test_update_todos_keeps_removed_completed_in_history confirming completed historical items are retained in metrics and that update_todos returns active items.
insights.lesson_learned: When changing state model (active vs historical) add tests asserting both return values and persisted state.

crates/forge_json_repair/src/schema_coer.short_description: Coerce JSON values to match JSON Schema types (used to repair/convert LLM outputs)
crates/forge_json_repair/src/schema_coer.description: Recursively coerces serde_json::Value instances to conform to schemars::Schema definitions, handling $ref resolution, nullable conversion, anyOf/oneOf/allOf, arrays, objects and string coercions to numbers/booleans/arrays. It includes permissive parsing (JSON, JSON5, and repair) to extract arrays or objects from messy LLM outputs and make function arguments schema-safe.

key_constructs.name: coerce_to_schema
key_constructs.purpose: Public entry to coerce a JSON Value into the expected Schema shape

key_constructs.name: coerce_value_with_schema
key_constructs.purpose: Recursive worker that dispatches coercion logic depending on schema shape

key_constructs.name: try_coerce_string
key_constructs.purpose: Attempt to coerce a string into integer, number, boolean, null, object or array

key_constructs.name: try_parse_json_string
key_constructs.purpose: Try parsing a string as JSON/JSON5 or apply json_repair fallback

key_constructs.name: extract_array_from_string
key_constructs.purpose: Permissively extract an array substring from noisy strings

key_behaviors: coerce and repair JSON outputs to match declared schemas
try multiple parsing strategies (JSON, JSON5, repair) for nested strings
handle nullable and combined-schema constructs

insights.title: Unwrap nested/double-encoded JSON strings and support JSON5/repair fallback
insights.problem: Strings containing JSON (and even double-encoded JSON) were not being recursively parsed; coercion to schema could miss the intended object.
insights.root_cause: try_parse_json_string only attempted serde_json then JSON5 previously; it did not unwrap nested JSON strings nor use json_repair fallback comprehensively.
insights.solution: Introduce parse_json_like_value which tries serde_json, then JSON5, then json_repair. try_parse_json_string now repeatedly unwraps up to 4 nested string layers (common double-encoding depth) to return the innermost structured value.

crates/forge_main/src/porcelain.rs.short_description: Convert Info into a tabular, machine-friendly Porcelain representation
crates/forge_main/src/porcelain.rs.description: Implements Porcelain, a flattened tabular representation of Info sections for machine-readable output and formatted display. It supports transformations like truncation, sorting, case conversion, long-form unpivoting, setting headers, and a Display impl to render aligned columns.

key_constructs.name: Porcelain
key_constructs.purpose: Core container for 2D optional string cells used to represent tabular info

key_constructs.name: Porcelain::from(&Info)
key_constructs.purpose: Convert Info/Section structures into the Porcelain table format

insights.title: Use char count (not byte len) for truncation to support multibyte Unicode
insights.problem: Previous truncation used byte-based slicing causing broken output or panics for multibyte characters (e.g., accented letters, emoji).
insights.root_cause: value.len() returned byte length; slicing assumed byte index equals character index.
insights.solution: Use value.chars().count() for length and value.chars().take(max_len).collect() to build truncated string and then append '...'. Updated tests for Unicode cases.
insights.lesson_learned: Text truncation must be character-aware (not byte-aware) in Rust when dealing with Unicode; tests for emojis and non-ASCII characters are critical.

insights.title: New Porcelain module centralizes machine-friendly output
insights.problem: Porcelain formatting was previously done ad-hoc in multiple UI locations
insights.root_cause: Multiple places implemented column formatting/skip/drop logic leading to duplication and inconsistent behavior
insights.solution: Add porcelain.rs providing Porcelain::from(&Info) and chaining API (.into_long(), .skip(n), .drop_col(n), .map_col) consumed by UI
insights.lesson_learned: Encapsulate machine-readable formatting in a dedicated module to keep UI logic declarative and consistent; it makes it easier to manage row/column transformations and skip semantics.

exercised_by: inline tests added in porcelain.rs (unicode truncation tests)

insights.title: Update command: run installer in background and support auto-update no-confirm
insights.problem: Users wanted background update flow and ability to auto-apply updates without interactive confirmation.
insights.root_cause: execute_update_command previously always prompted to close the app after update.
insights.solution: execute_update_command now accepts auto_update bool to immediately exit after successful update when requested. UI routes TopLevelCommand::Update to on_update with Update.auto_update flag. Tests added for CLI parsing of --no-confirm flag.
insights.lesson_learned: Background update flows need to respect non-interactive contexts; expose a no-confirm flag and ensure update task spawns without blocking UI.

insights.lesson_learned: Keep external URLs centralized (README and update command) and update across code/docs together.

insights.title: Switch active agent to in-memory session state
insights.problem: Previously setting the active agent updated persistent app config which made the 'active agent' global across sessions and required disk writes for what should be a session-level choice.
insights.root_cause: Design stored active agent in app_config and persisted it; switching agents required writing config frequently.
insights.solution: Add active_agent_id: RwLock<Option<AgentId>> to AgentLoaderService/AgentRegistry to keep active agent in-memory (session-scoped). Update get_active_agent/get_active_agent_id/set_active_agent to read/write the RwLock. Remove reliance on AppConfig for immediate active agent resolution. This avoids constant config writes and isolates sessions.

insights.title: Add and integrate rate limiter to prevent event dispatch loops
insights.problem: Unbounded event dispatch during failures could cause runaway loops (e.g., write errors triggering events that cause more errors).
insights.root_cause: No upper bound on event emission; errors in the event path could cause flood of events.
insights.solution: Add RateLimiter and use it in Tracker::dispatch to drop events when rate exceeded. Later commits adjusted ownership and synchronization of the limiter (Arc<Mutex<RateLimiter>> and use of inc_and_check) to avoid races.

insights.title: Add provider-specific transformers: Kimi K2 reasoning flat-field, strict tool schema enforcement for OpenCode Zen
insights.problem: Different model backends expect different request shapes (kimi_k2 expects reasoning_content flat string; opencode_zen via OpenAI backend needs nullable enum converted to anyOf with null).
insights.root_cause: Heterogeneous provider expectations for reasoning and JSON schema encoding.
insights.solution: Added KimiK2Reasoning transformer, EnforceStrictToolSchema transformer and integrated them into ProviderPipeline for provider-specific routing (when model/provider match). Added tests. Also wired reasoning_content field into request DTO.
insights.lesson_learned: Maintain a pipeline of pluggable transformers to adapt to provider idiosyncrasies. Keep mapping decisions centralized in the pipeline.

insights.title: Enable caching for minimax models via open_router pipeline
insights.problem: Minimax models proxied through open_router didn't receive caching transformer (SetCache) causing potential redundant calls and performance loss.
insights.root_cause: SetCache.when predicate didn't include minimax in the when_model regex.
insights.solution: Updated pipeline to include 'minimax' in when_model list and added tests verifying cache flag application for minimax models when provider supports open_router params.
insights.lesson_learned: When using regex-based model matching for transformer application, explicitly include all vendor/model name variants (minimax) and add pipeline unit tests to prevent regressions.
